input.title: Logistics and Shipping Platform
input.description: Logistics and Shipping Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

modules: Rate Calculation by Weight and Distance
scope: Rate Calculation by Weight and Distance workflows
Barcode and QR Code Tracking workflows
constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Shipment Booking
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete shipment booking according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving shipment booking changes.
Shipment Booking must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for shipment booking.

FR-01.title: Plan Shipment Booking Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for shipment booking and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete shipment booking according to the approved business workflow; The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving shipment booking changes; Shipment Booking must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for shipment booking is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for shipment booking are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for shipment booking can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Shipment Booking Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for shipment booking on React, Go, PostgreSQL, Redis. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete shipment booking according to the approved business workflow; The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving shipment booking changes; Shipment Booking must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
acceptance_criteria: Core shipment booking business rules are implemented for Logistics and Shipping Platform
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for shipment booking
API/service behavior for shipment booking is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Shipment Booking Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for shipment booking with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The shipment booking user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for shipment booking behave correctly
The shipment booking UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Shipment Booking Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for shipment booking. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for shipment booking supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for shipment booking are defined correctly
Migrations for shipment booking can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Harden Shipment Booking Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to shipment booking. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for shipment booking are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in shipment booking are audited and protected against misuse

FR-01.title: Build Shipment Booking Reporting and KPI Logic
FR-01.description: Implement the reporting layer for shipment booking, including aggregate queries, KPI calculations, filters, drill-down behavior, and export-ready payloads.
